title: Yesterday, AB 2949 by Assemblymember Alex Lee (D-San José) was signed into law by Governor Newsom. The bill exempts vehicles registered to US military veterans (including disabled
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: Tolls can be incredibly cost-prohibitive throughout the Bay Area and Southern California.
The financial burden of toll roads can lead to significant economic impacts on individuals who must use them.
Disabled veterans may find it more difficult to attain a job or seek ongoing medical care due to financial obstacles.
Nearly three in ten disabled veterans report that their impairments have prevented them from getting or keeping a job.
Two-thirds of disabled veterans who are 100% disabled say their impairments have prevented them from obtaining a job.
The signing of AB 2949 will provide financial relief to veterans by exempting them from toll costs.
